channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Doda’s Thatri faces damage from flash flood and landslide; NH-244 closed
item.description: A severe flash flood and landslide have wreaked havoc in Doda’s Thatri region of Jammu and Kashmir, causing significant damage to infrastructure and vehicles. The natural disaster has led to the closu...

item.title: Argentina pulls off dramatic comeback against Egypt in World Cup clash
item.description: In a thrilling World Cup last-16 match, Argentina staged a remarkable comeback against Egypt after trailing 2-0. Lionel Messi was pivotal in the turnaround, scoring and assisting crucial goals as his ...

item.title: Almajiri Commission Faces Scrutiny Over Road Funding Outside Educational Mandate
item.description: The National Commission for Almajiri and Out-of-School Children Education (NCAOOSCE) is under fire after being allocated ₦8.4 billion for road infrastructure projects in Ogun, Ekiti, and Katsina state...

item.title: Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Duterte: Prosecution's Strategy and Legal Challenges
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte continued on July 7, 2026, with the prosecution team claiming they 'scored points' by presenting videos of Duterte making death threats against Pre...

item.title: Japanese Probe Hayabusa 2 Completes Successful Flyby of Asteroid Torifune
item.description: The Japanese space agency JAXA's Hayabusa 2 probe successfully conducted a flyby of the asteroid 98943 Torifune on July 5, 2026, at a relative speed of 5.3 km/s. This mission marked the second asteroi...

item.title: Belgium Defeats USA in World Cup Match Amid Controversy Over Balogun's Participation
item.description: Belgium secured a dominant 4-1 victory over the United States in their World Cup clash, with captain Youri Tielemans revealing the team held a meeting after FIFA's controversial decision to reinstate ...

item.title: President Tinubu Directs ICPC to Investigate Fake PFIPC and Related Activities
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has directed the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) to investigate the activities of a fictitious 'Presidential Foreign Intervention...

item.title: The Link Between Sleep Patterns and Digestive Health
item.description: The article explores the connection between sleep schedules and digestive health, particularly focusing on constipation. It highlights research indicating that insufficient sleep is linked to constipa...

item.title: Historical Impeachment Cases in the Philippines
item.description: The article details key impeachment cases in Philippine history, highlighting the processes and outcomes of high-profile officials. Former President Joseph Estrada was the first impeached for alleged ...

item.title: FNB Funds Balwin's R470m Rental Housing Project in Johannesburg
item.description: FNB Commercial Property Finance has committed R470m to fund Balwin Properties’ new rental housing development, The Klulee, in Linbro Park, Johannesburg. The project will be built in three phases, with...

item.title: Ghana and South Africa Postpone Diplomatic Meeting Over Xenophobic Tensions
item.description: The governments of Ghana and South Africa have postponed their planned meeting amid a diplomatic rift triggered by anti-immigration protests in South Africa. Ghana's President John Mahama has uninvite...

item.title: Carney makes first Senate appointments, names Richard Martel and changes Senate selection criteria
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has announced his first Senate appointments since taking office, naming former Conservative MP Richard Martel, principal secretary Tom Pitfield, physician Dr. Rodeny Ouellet...

item.title: New State Election Commissioner N. Seshadrinathan Appointed Amid Controversy
item.description: N. Seshadrinathan has been appointed as the State Election Commissioner (SEC) of Kerala, succeeding the previous incumbent. A retired Principal Sessions Judge of Lakshadweep, he has served in various ...

item.title: Government Launches Gwa Kiongo Dam Project to Boost Agriculture in Nyandarua
item.description: The Kenyan government has announced the revival of the Gwa Kiongo Dam Irrigation Project, set to irrigate 500 acres and benefit over 1,000 farmers. Principal Secretary for Irrigation Ephantus Kimotho ...

item.title: Australia and China's Diplomatic Tensions Escalate Over Missile Test
item.description: The Albanese government has navigated Australia-China relations more effectively than its predecessor, normalising trade and strengthening Pacific alliances. However, recent tensions have emerged foll...

item.title: DeepSeek Expands into Semiconductor Development with Inference-Focused AI Chip
item.description: Chinese startup DeepSeek is advancing its strategic shift by developing a specialized AI chip designed for inference tasks, according to three sources familiar with the project. This move aims to redu...

item.title: PSC dismisses 4 senior officers, demotes others in disciplinary action
item.description: The Police Service Commission (PSC) has sacked four senior officers and demoted others for gross misconduct and unprofessional behavior. Chairman Hashimu Argungu revealed during a press briefing in Ab...

item.title: New Afrikaans Series Highlights Western Cape's Marine Protected Areas
item.description: A new Afrikaans television series titled 'Hey Skipper' is set to premiere on DStv Channel 144 kykNET, showcasing the marine protected areas (MPAs) of the Western Cape. Produced by adventurer Dewald Vi...

item.title: Bunda Town's Motorcycle Taxi Drivers Maintain Daily Income
item.description: Motorcycle taxi operators, known as 'bodaboda' riders, remain central to Bunda town's transportation system despite challenges. These riders provide essential short-distance travel services, filling g...

item.title: Tanzanian Court Rules on Chadema's Financial Resources Case
item.description: A Tanzanian court has ruled on a high-profile case involving the financial resources of Chadema, the country's main opposition party. The case centered on allegations regarding the management and sour...
